title: fagraea crassifolia
intro: Fagraea crassifolia, also known as the sweet olive or fragrant olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is a shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers and edible berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of Fagraea crassifolia can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are said to have health benefits such as reducing stress, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system.
